name: flydra-core
documentation:
# flydra_core - core component of flydra

This directory houses the source code for the `flydra_core` Python package.

For more information about flydra, see [here](https://github.com/strawlab/flydra).

classes.name: flydra_core.kalman.ekf.EKF
documentation:
Extendend Kalman Filter

This is only a "half" EKF - only the observation model is
non-linear.  This non-linear function h() maps the a priori
estimate of position into camera coordinates for N cameras.

documentation:
Sometimes the Kalman tracker will not gobble all the points
it should. This still prevents spawning a new Kalman

methods.name: flydra_core.kalman.flydra_tracker.Tracker.remove_duplicate_detections
documentation: remove points that are close to current objects being tracked

Handle multiple tracked objects using TrackedObject instances.

This class keeps a list of objects currently being tracked. It
also keeps a couple other lists for dealing with cases when the
tracked objects are no longer 'live'.

functions.name: flydra_core.geom.cross
documentation: see http://web.mit.edu/thouis/pluecker.txt

functions.name: flydra_core.geom.line_from_points
documentation:
create PlueckerLine instance given 2 distinct points

example2:

```pycon
>>> p1 = ThreeTuple((2.0, 3.0, 7.0))
>>> p2 = ThreeTuple((2.0, 1.0, 0.0))
>>> L = line_from_points(p1,p2)
>>> print(L)
PlueckerLine(ThreeTuple((0.0,2.0,7.0)),ThreeTuple((-7.0,14.0,-4.0)))
```

```pycon
>>> q1 = ThreeTuple((0.0, 2.0, 7.0))
>>> q2 = ThreeTuple((0.0, 2.0, 0.0))
>>> L2 = line_from_points(q1,q2)
>>> print(L2.dist2())
4.0
>>> print(L2.closest())
ThreeTuple((0.0,2.0,-0.0))
```

functions.name: flydra_core.geom.line_from_HZline
documentation: line from Hartley & Zisserman Pluecker coordinates

classes.name: flydra_core.geom.ThreeTuple

methods.name: flydra_core.geom.ThreeTuple.cross
documentation:
cross product

# Parameters

other

: The other point to do the cross product with.

# Returns

result

: The cross product result

# Examples

```pycon
>>> x = ThreeTuple((1,0,0))
>>> y = ThreeTuple((0,1,0))
>>> x.cross(y)
ThreeTuple((0,0,1))
```

methods.name: flydra_core.geom.ThreeTuple.dot
documentation:
dot product

# Parameters

other

: The other point to do the dot product with.

# Returns

result

: The dot product result

# Examples

```pycon
>>> x = ThreeTuple((1,0,0))
>>> y = ThreeTuple((0,1,0))
>>> x.dot(y)
0
```

methods.name: flydra_core.geom.ThreeTuple.dist_from
documentation:
get distance from other point

# Parameters

other

: The other point to find the distance from

# Returns

result

: The distance to the other point

# Examples

```pycon
>>> x = ThreeTuple((1,0,0))
>>> y = ThreeTuple((0,1,0))
>>> x.dist_from(y) == np.sqrt(2)
True
```

A tuple of 3 points.

Parameters
----------
vals : {sequence, ThreeTuple instance}
  The tuple of 3 points

classes.name: flydra_core.geom.PlueckerLine

methods.name: flydra_core.geom.PlueckerLine.get_my_point_closest_to_line
documentation:
find point on line closest to other line

# Parameters

other

: The line to find closest point relative to

# Returns

pt

: The point closest to other line

# Examples

```pycon
>>> # A line along +y going through (1,0,0)
>>> a = ThreeTuple((1,0,0))
>>> b = ThreeTuple((1,1,0))
>>> line = line_from_points(a,b)
```

```pycon
>>> # A line along +z going through (0,0,0)
>>> O = ThreeTuple((0,0,0))
>>> z = ThreeTuple((0,0,1))
>>> zaxis = line_from_points(z,O)
```

```pycon
>>> # The closest point between them:
>>> line.get_my_point_closest_to_line( zaxis )
ThreeTuple((1.0,0.0,0.0))
```

methods.name: flydra_core.geom.PlueckerLine.dist2
documentation: return minimum squared distance from origin

methods.name: flydra_core.geom.PlueckerLine.closest
documentation:
return point on line closest to origin
Examples
\--------
\>>> a = ThreeTuple((1.0, 0.0, 0.0))
\>>> b = ThreeTuple((1.0, 1.0, 0.0))
\>>> line = line_from_points(a,b)
\>>> line.closest()
ThreeTuple((1.0,0.0,-0.0))

a line in 3D space

Parameters
----------
u : ThreeTuple instance
   direction of line
v : ThreeTuple instance
   cross product of 2 points on line

classes.name: flydra_core.geom.LineSegment

methods.name: flydra_core.geom.LineSegment.__init__
documentation: create LineSegment instance given endpoints

part of a line between 2 endpoints

```pycon
>>> seg = LineSegment(ThreeTuple((0,0,0)),ThreeTuple((0,0,10)))
```

```pycon
>>> point = ThreeTuple((1,0,5))
>>> print(seg.get_distance_from_point(point))
1.0
```

```pycon
>>> point = ThreeTuple((0,0,-1))
>>> print(seg.get_distance_from_point(point))
1.0
```

```pycon
>>> point = ThreeTuple((2,0,0))
>>> print(seg.get_distance_from_point(point))
2.0
```

functions.name: flydra_core.reconstruct.lineIntersect3D
documentation:
Find intersection point of lines in 3D space, in the least squares sense.

PA :          Nx3-matrix containing starting point of N lines
PB :          Nx3-matrix containing end point of N lines
P_Intersect : Best intersection point of the N lines, in least squares sense.
distances   : Distances from intersection point to the input lines
Anders Eikenes, 2012
translated to Python by Andrew Straw 2015

methods.name: flydra_core.reconstruct.SingleCameraCalibration.get_3D_plane_and_ray
documentation:
return a ray and plane defined by a point and a slope at that point

The 2D distorted point (x0d, y0d) is transfored to a 3D
ray. Together with the slope at that point, a 3D plane that
passes through the 2D point in the direction of the slope is
returned.

# Parameters

: identifying camera

Pmat

: camera calibration matrix

res

: resolution (width,height)

helper

: (optional) specifies camera distortion parameters

methods.name: flydra_core.reconstruct.Reconstructor.get_scaled
documentation: return a copy of self. (DEPRECATED.)

methods.name: flydra_core.reconstruct.Reconstructor.save_to_h5file
documentation: create groups with calibration information

methods.name: flydra_core.reconstruct.Reconstructor.find3d
documentation:
Find 3D coordinate using all data given

Implements a linear triangulation method to find a 3D
point. For example, see Hartley & Zisserman section 12.2
(p.312). Also, 12.8 for intersecting lines.

Note: For a function which does hyptothesis testing to
selectively choose 2D to incorporate, see
hypothesis_testing_algorithm__find_best_3d() in
reconstruct_utils.

This function can optionally undistort points.

methods.name: flydra_core.reconstruct.Reconstructor.find2d
documentation:
find projection of 3D points in X onto 2D image plan for cam_id

for rank1 case:
  X : shape==(3,) single point, or shape==(4,) homogeneous single point
  returns shape==(2,) projection
else:
  X : (N,4) array of homogeneous points
  returns shape==(2,N) projection

methods.name: flydra_core.reconstruct.Reconstructor.find3d_single_cam
documentation: see also SingleCameraCalibration.get_example_3d_point_creating_image_point()

methods.name: flydra_core.reconstruct.Reconstructor.get_projected_line_from_2d
documentation:
project undistorted points into pluecker line

see also SingleCameraCalibration.get_example_3d_point_creating_image_point()

methods.name: flydra_core.reconstruct.Reconstructor.add_element
documentation: add self as XML element to parent

A complete calibration for all cameras in a flydra setup

# Parameters

cal_source : {string, list, open pytables file instance}

> The source of the calibration. Can be a string specifying the
> path of a directory output by MultiCamSelfCal, a string
> specifying an .xml calibration file path, a string specifying a
> pytables file, a list of {class}`SingleCameraCalibration`
> instances, or an open pytables file object.

do_normalize_pmat

: Whether the pmat is normalized such that the intrinsic parameters
  are in the expected form

minimum_eccentricity

: Minimum eccentricity (ratio of long to short axis of an
  ellipse) of 2D detected object required to use detected
  orientation for performing 3D body orientation estimation.

functions.name: flydra_core.undistort.reference_rect
documentation:
arguments:

I is image

optional arguments:

R is 3x3 rotation (of affine transformation) matrix, defaults to eye(3)
f is focal length (horizontal and vertical), defaults to (1,1)
c is image center (horizontal and vertical), defaults to (0,0)
k is nonlinear parameters, defaults to (0,0,0,0,0)

methods.name: flydra_core.undistort.CachedUndistorter.rect
documentation:
arguments:

I is image

optional arguments:

R is 3x3 rotation (of affine transformation) matrix, defaults to eye(3)
f is focal length (horizontal and vertical), defaults to (1,1)
c is image center (horizontal and vertical), defaults to (0,0)
k is nonlinear parameters, defaults to (0,0,0,0,0)
